ses EventEmitter. * You can register handlers for events 'open', 'message', 'close', or 'error'. * The event 'open' will be emitted when the socket has been successfully opened. * T
ocket has been successfully opened. * The event 'message' will be emitted with the body of the message as an * argument when an incoming message arrives on the socket. * You can invoke th
* You can invoke the send() function to send data to the server. * * The type of data sent and receive
* The type of data sent and received can be specified with the 'sendType' * and 'receiveType' options. * In principle, any MIME type can be specified, but

In principle, any MIME type can be specified, but the host may support only * a subset of MIME types. The client and the server have to agree on the type, * or the data will not get through correctly.

parsed as JSON, * and if the parsing fails, will be provided as a string interpretation of the byte * stream. * * __text/\*__: Any text type is sent
